summary: Fake OpenAI-compatible provider E2E is certified for provider-specific tool-call quirks, tool-result reply formatting, redacted archives, and configured fallback recovery.

claims: Compact provider-specific execute_command calls are normalized into argv-based execution plans.
Native tool sessions use provider_api execution path with tool_first_native invocation.
Final operator replies keep tool results and strip provider/runtime boilerplate.
A saturated fake primary Google provider produces a queue retry with an explicit fallback route.
The fake OpenRouter-compatible fallback completes the run and delivers the recovered answer.
The operator-visible answer omits the primary provider saturation error.

redaction.localReport: Local report keeps a redacted Vitest output preview only.
redaction.trackedArchive: Tracked archive omits provider HTTP payload contents, API keys, bearer tokens, and full test logs.
